# GitHub Copilot Instructions

This file contains behavioral rules and guidance for using GitHub Copilot in this project.

## Behavioral Rules

- Use Copilot to suggest code that follows project coding standards and best practices
- Prefer modular, readable, and maintainable code
- Avoid generating code with hardcoded secrets or credentials
- Ensure suggestions align with the documentation in the appropriate category:
  - For backend requests, refer to files in `docs/backend/`
  - For frontend requests, refer to files in `docs/frontend/`

## Styling Guidelines

- **Always use shadcn/ui components** for UI elements (buttons, cards, forms, etc.)
- **Never create component-scoped CSS files** - maintain all styles globally through:
  - Tailwind CSS utility classes
  - shadcn/ui component variants
  - Global CSS variables defined in `src/index.css` or similar
- **Use Tailwind utility classes** for component styling, not inline styles or CSS modules
- Keep styling consistent and centralized to ensure reusability across all components

## Usage Guidance

- Review Copilot suggestions before accepting
- Edit and adapt generated code to fit project requirements
- For backend code, reference:
  - `docs/backend/architecture.md`
  - `docs/backend/development-guide.md`
  - `docs/backend/coding-standards.md`
  - `docs/backend/testing-practices.md`
- For frontend code, reference:
  - `docs/frontend/architecture.md`
  - `docs/frontend/development-guide.md`
  - `docs/frontend/coding-standards.md`
  - `docs/frontend/testing-practices.md`
